summary: Italian composer and pianist (b. 1955) known for minimalist, ambient piano music. His piece 'Nuvole Bianche' has been featured in many films and TV shows. His album Nightbook accompanies piano with synthesized sounds. His second album, Stanze, was written for harpist Cecilia Chailly. Associated with the ambient music genre alongside Brian Eno and Max Richter.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Italian minimalist composer and pianist. Best known for ambient, repetitive piano works often used in film and television. The Guardian controversially called his 2019 Barbican concert performance 'as cliched and shameless as a Simon Cowell No. 1.' Associated with ambient music alongside Brian Eno and Max Richter. Wikipedia

clues.clue: 'Nuvole Bianche' is his most famous piece, featured in many films and TV shows
examples: Name this Italian composer whose piece 'Nuvole Bianche' has been featured in many films and TV shows.

clues.clue: Album Nightbook accompanies piano with synthesized sounds; album Seven Days Walking reviewed by The Guardian as 'as cliched and shameless as a Simon Cowell No. 1' (2019 Barbican concert)
examples: In 2019, The Guardian reviewed this pianist's performance of his album Seven Days Walking at the Barbican Center and claimed that he was 'as cliched and shameless as a Simon Cowell No. 1.' He accompanied his playing with synthesized sounds on his album Nightbook.

clues.clue: Second album Stanze was written for harpist Cecilia Chailly
examples: Einaudi's second album, Stanze, was written for Cecilia Chailly, a player of this instrument [harp].

clues.clue: Associated with ambient music (alongside Brian Eno and Max Richter); genre characterized by slow, repetitive music meant to evoke calm backgrounds
examples: Many of Einaudi's compositions are considered part of this genre [ambient music] of slow and repetitive music meant to evoke or accompany particular backgrounds.
Brian Eno and Ludovico Einaudi are key musicians in this genre of music, inspired by the 'furniture music' of Erik Satie.

comprehensive_summary: Ludovico Einaudi (born 1955) is an Italian composer and pianist known for minimalist, ambient piano music. His piece 'Nuvole Bianche' has been featured in many films and television shows, making it his best-known work. His album Nightbook accompanies his piano playing with synthesized sounds. His 2015 album Seven Days Walking was performed at the Barbican Center in 2019, where a Guardian review called it 'as cliched and shameless as a Simon Cowell No. 1.' His second album, Stanze, was written specifically for harpist Cecilia Chailly. Einaudi is considered a key figure in the ambient music genre alongside Brian Eno and Max Richter — a genre characterized by slow, repetitive music meant to evoke a sense of calm, inspired by the 'furniture music' concept of Erik Satie.

Composer: associated with ambient music alongside Brian Eno and Max Richter; second album Stanze written for harpist Cecilia Chailly
